items.title: Colorectal cancer warning signs younger adults should never ignore

items.title: Lab-grown mini brains may predict which Alzheimer’s treatments will work
items.summary: Miniature brain models grown from patients’ cells revealed striking differences in how Alzheimer’s-related tissue responds to an antidepressant. The organoids and the particles they release could eventually guide more personalized treatments and provide new clues for diagnosing the disease.
